text:
Market today: AI infrastructure spending is the dominant theme again - 'follow the money' into hardware/chips, not software. Headlines on data centers, Nvidia, SK Hynix, and ARM-linked AI plays are keeping chip names bid. Your universe is directly in the crosshairs of this rotation.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest concern - it's a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF, decay is eating you alive, and on margin the damage compounds fast; stop at $200 is your line. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, leveraged, and already down hard - that stop at $21.38 is razor-thin and nearly triggered. SMH (-2.5%) is softer than the AI buzz suggests; watch whether it follows chips higher or stays lagging.

Watch: Whether SMH/SOXL actually catch a bid from the AI hardware narrative today, or keep diverging from the headlines. Also watch SPAL's $21.38 stop - it's inches away.

Bottom line: Your two leveraged ETFs are bleeding on margin - stops are your only friends right now, respect them without hesitation.
plays: SPAL: Stop at $21.38 is nearly touched. If it prints there, exit immediately - leveraged, decaying, and already -13.7% with no room to breathe on margin.
SOXL: -14.5% with decay accelerating. If price approaches $200 stop, honor it - 3x leverage on margin in a choppy tape is a fast way to a much larger hole.
No new buys today - AI hardware buzz is real but SMH is still soft; wait for your chip names to confirm before adding any new leveraged exposure.
